steps.text: Thoroughly test the method
Peer review the method
Publish the method as an EPA method
Peer review all sampling methods for chemical and radiochemical parameters.
Follow the emergency response method validation policy (Agency Policy Directive Number FEM-2010-01).
Review and consult sampling method guidance documents and procedures to determine the level of validation associated with the method.
Indicate whether the method uses a physical sampling or collection tool/technique or whether the method is a field measurement process.
Specify conditions for either type of method.
Conduct the initial controlled validation test.
Test multiple locations and conditions in controlled environments.
Troubleshoot and optimize the method if difficulties are revealed.
Repeat some or all aspects of the study.
Troubleshoot and optimize the method after conducting the study, if necessary.
Clearly describe the components of a field sampling technique or field measurement processes subject to validation.
Define the matrix being sampled and the purpose of the sampling effort.
Ensure sampling methods and validation plans cover all aspects of the sampling event from sample collection through delivery.
Clearly define all aspects of equipment/instrument set-up, calibration, operation, sample preparation, sample cleanup, and QA/QC checks.
Perform calibration, routine maintenance, and at least annual standardization or certification on all instruments used to measure or analyze samples in the field.
Calibrate the instrument following procedures in the manufacturer's users' manual, performed in the laboratory, or as defined by the method.
Certify and/or calibrate equipment used to obtain sample volumetric and/or weight measurements (or instruments incorporating them) with prescriptive or performance-based specifications.
Estimate method bias by measuring materials whose composition is reasonably well known, comparing results to at least one alternate method or procedure, or analyzing spiked materials.
Define the term 'detection limit' and provide a description of how it was evaluated during method validation.
Verify limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models by testing materials containing the analyte at the claimed detection level.
Address detection limit as it applies to instrument detection capability.
Determine and collect the minimum sample amount (volume/weight) required to support analysis of a single sample based on target analytes, analytical method, and data requirements.
Provide an overall summary of the validation report in the Summary section.
Provide background information on sampling method development and state the purpose of the method in the Introduction section.

Describe the sampling method validation technique/experimental design, including a description of the test method/procedure and details of equipment/locations used with calibration status in the Methodology section.
Discuss how the sampling method scope and applicability served to define the range of method performance in the Methodology section.
Include a discussion of the number or items or the quantity constituting a sample, as appropriate for the method, whether the samples are composites or grab.

Address safety concerns and procedures, including personal protective equipment and first aid, physical hazards, and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.
Provide a discussion of the defined quality control/quality assurance (QA/QC) checks and guidances.
Discuss method development.
Discuss results of the validation testing and an evaluation including comparison with the reference substances and preparations, acceptance criteria and recommendations.
Note any changes made to the sampling method as a result of the validation studies.
Discuss formal acceptance/rejection of work.
Provide recommendations for future work.
Integrate validation planning into initial planning (e.g., directed planning process).
Include the sampling validation plan as a section within the plan or as a stand-alone document attached as an appendix.
Address the required information in the sampling validation plan.
Communicate the information and documentation identified in the validation plans to the laboratory as part of the Statement of Work (SOW).

preconditions.text: Prior to publication of sampling methods
In emergency response situations (e.g., natural disaster, homeland security)
Procedural changes are required after troubleshooting/optimization
Field measurement processes are to be performed
Plan how in-depth uncertainty is to be determined based upon data quality objectives (DQOs) before samples are collected in the field.
Limits are derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models.

Before publication, EPA sampling methods for chemical and radiochemical parameters are either peer reviewed in accordance with the most current version of the EPA Science Technology Policy Council’s Peer Review Handbook (1) or reviewed by a documented alternate procedure.

constraints.text: Any determination of uncertainty or bias of an environmental measurement must be made in relation to both the analytical method and the sampling method for chemical and radiochemical parameters.
Ensure that the new collection method is consistent with the planned analytical methods to be utilized.
Document and either limit the variables tested or ensure that a range of conditions are included in the test.
Document the process by which the samples are collected.
Control and document variability to determine the range for purposeful uses and identify techniques to be utilized when using the sampling technology.
Safety concerns and procedures, including personal protective equipment and first aid, must be addressed in both the sampling method and the validation plan.
Address physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.
Ensure the appropriate method performance characteristics by the instrument at the time of the analysis.
Typical detection limits claimed by the manufacturer are not always achievable in the field due to environmental conditions, sample type, etc.
Quantitation limits only apply to screening or direct reading methods.
At the minimum, the sampling method validation report must address the information contained in Table 1.

The sampling validation plan must address: 1. Purpose of Validation, 2. Sampling Procedure (including principle, test procedures, equipment, variables, samples, efficiencies, QC), 3. Field Variances, 4. Data Validation, and 5. Performance Characteristics.
